name: Template Rendering Engine
description: Implement a template rendering engine across two modules. Module lexer.py must define: TokenType enum with values TEXT, VAR, IF, ELSE, ENDIF, FOR, ENDFOR; Token dataclass with fields type (TokenType), value (str); tokenize(template) -> list[Token] function that parses templates with {{ var }} for variables, {% if condition %}, {% else %}, {% endif %} for conditionals, and {% for item in collection %}, {% endfor %} for loops. Module renderer.py must import from lexer.py and implement TemplateEngine class with method render(template, context) -> str that tokenizes then renders the template. Variable lookup supports dot notation (e.g. user.name). Missing variables render as empty string. For loops iterate over lists in context. If blocks evaluate truthiness of context variable.
test_imports: from lexer import tokenize, Token, TokenType
signature:
# lexer.py
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum

class TokenType(Enum):
    TEXT = 'TEXT'
    VAR = 'VAR'
    IF = 'IF'
    ELSE = 'ELSE'
    ENDIF = 'ENDIF'
    FOR = 'FOR'
    ENDFOR = 'ENDFOR'

@dataclass
class Token:
    type: TokenType
    value: str

def tokenize(template: str) -> list[Token]: ...

# renderer.py
from lexer import tokenize, Token, TokenType

class TemplateEngine:
    def render(self, template: str, context: dict) -> str: ...
tests:
def test_variable_substitution():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    result = engine.render('Hello {{ name }}!', {'name': 'Alice'})
    assert result == 'Hello Alice!'

def test_dot_notation():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    result = engine.render('{{ user.name }}', {'user': {'name': 'Bob'}})
    assert result == 'Bob'

def test_missing_variable():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    result = engine.render('Hi {{ name }}!', {})
    assert result == 'Hi !'

def test_if_true():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    result = engine.render('{% if show %}Visible{% endif %}', {'show': True})
    assert result == 'Visible'

def test_if_false_with_else():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    result = engine.render('{% if logged_in %}Welcome{% else %}Please login{% endif %}', {'logged_in': False})
    assert result == 'Please login'

def test_for_loop():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    result = engine.render('{% for item in items %}{{ item }} {% endfor %}', {'items': ['a', 'b', 'c']})
    assert result == 'a b c '
constraints: {{ var }} substitutes variable from context, supports dot notation
{% if var %} evaluates truthiness of context variable
{% for item in collection %} iterates over list, binding each element
Missing variables render as empty string, no errors
Tokenizer handles arbitrary text between template tags
renderer.py imports from lexer.py and exports TemplateEngine
